channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Israel's prime minister says Trump has invited him to the White House on Feb. 4
item.description: WADI GAZA, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said Tuesday that President Donald Trump has invited him to visit the White House on Feb. 4, which would make him the first foreign leader to do so in Trump’s second term.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: The discovery of brutal mass graves in Syria reveals Assad's legacy of horror
item.description: Syria’s Civil Defense has uncovered over two dozen charred bodies in two basements in rural Damascus, adding to the growing tally of mass graves discovered since the ousting of Bashar Assad.
item.dc:creator: Omar Albam And Sally Abou Aljoud, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: US ships return to Red Sea amid Gaza ceasefire
item.description: U.S. ships are returning to the Red Sea following promises from Yemen’s Houthi rebels to abstain from attacks on American and British vessels. The pledge, which comes after more than a year of shipping traffic disruption on a major international trade route, was made on Jan.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: China built out record amount of wind and solar power in 2024
item.description: China raced ahead building renewable energy last year, installing more wind and solar power than ever before and continuing to leave all other countries in the dust.
item.dc:creator: Isabella O'Malley, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Senate Democrats block GOP's ICC sanctions bill
item.description: Senate Democrats on Tuesday blocked Republicans from advancing legislation to sanction the International Criminal Court, arguing the bill had sweeping consequences on allies and American businesses.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Middle East latest: Palestinians return to their homes in northern Gaza for a second day
item.description: Crowds of displaced Palestinians made the arduous journey back to heavily destroyed northern Gaza for a second day, under a fragile ceasefire between Israel and Hamas.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: South African elephant kills tourist who was trying to save children at Kruger National Park
item.description: A tourist at South Africa's Kruger National Park was trampled to death by an elephant as he was trying to protect children, officials say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Serbia's prime minister resigns as anti-corruption protests sparked by deadly collapse swell
item.description: Serbia’s populist prime minister, Milos Vucevic, has resigned in an attempt to calm political tensions stoked by weeks of massive anti-corruption protests sparked by the deadly collapse of a concrete overhang at a recently renovated train station.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Italy sends 49 more migrants to Albania for processing following earlier court rejections
item.description: SHENGJIN, Albania (AP) — An Italian navy ship arrived Tuesday in Albania with 49 migrants intercepted in international waters for processing of their asylum applications at special Albanian centers, in the third such attempt following hurdles earlier raised by Italian courts.
item.dc:creator: ERION XHABAFTI and LLAZAR SEMINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trains canceled across Bangladesh as rail union goes on strike
item.description: DHAKA, Bangladesh (AP) — Trains were canceled across Bangladesh on Tuesday as railway staff went on strike for higher pensions and other benefits, affecting tens of thousands of passengers and freight transport.
item.dc:creator: JULHAS ALAM, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Palestinians celebrate their return to northern Gaza after 15 months of war
item.description: WADI GAZA, Gaza Strip (AP) — Hundreds of thousands of Palestinians streamed into Gaza’s most heavily destroyed area on Monday after Israel opened the north for the first time since the early weeks of the war with Hamas, a dramatic reversal of their exodus 15 months ago.
item.dc:creator: ABDEL KAREEM HANA, WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: CDC ordered to stop working with WHO immediately, upending expectations of an extended withdrawal
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — U.S. public health officials have been told to stop working with the World Health Organization, effective immediately. A U.S.
item.dc:creator: MIKE STOBBE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: EU official: Europe 'not negotiating' on Greenland
item.description: European Union foreign policy chief Kaja Kallas said Monday that Europe is “not negotiating” with the United States over control of Greenland, as President Trump continues to insist that acquiring the self-governing territory of Denmark is necessary for American national security.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Northern Gaza is shattered. The spirit of returning Palestinians is not
item.description: WADI GAZA, Gaza Strip (AP) — They walked for hours loaded with whatever clothes, food and blankets they could carry. Many smiled, some hugged loved ones they hadn’t seen for months. Hundreds of thousands of Palestinians filled Gaza’s main coastal road as they streamed back to homes in the north.
item.dc:creator: OSAMA SALEH, ABDEL-KAREEM HANA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: After new CIA finding, China says 'extremely unlikely' COVID-19 came from a lab
item.description: China on Monday dismissed the possibility that the virus that caused COVID-19 leaked from a lab, after the CIA said it now favors the so-called lab leak theory over natural transmission.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, The Hill

item.title: Belarus opposition and Western leaders reject election result that extends strongman's rule
item.description: Belarus' opposition activists and Western officials on Monday denounced an orchestrated election that extends the more than three-decade rule of authoritarian President Alexander Lukashenko. The country's exiled opposition leader called the result “sheer nonsense.
item.dc:creator: YURAS KARMANAU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Preliminary report on South Korean plane crash confirms bird strike, but no cause yet
item.description: The preliminary accident report released by South Korea's Aviation and Railway Accident Investigation Board on Monday said that feathers and bird blood stains were found in both engines.
item.dc:creator: Hyung-jin Kim, WGN 9

item.title: Preliminary report on South Korean plane crash confirms bird strike, but no cause yet
item.description: The preliminary accident report released by South Korea's Aviation and Railway Accident Investigation Board on Monday said that feathers and bird blood stains were found in both engines.
item.dc:creator: Hyung-jin Kim, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Airliner's final 4 minutes of recordings are missing after crash that killed 179: investigators
item.description: The black boxes of a plane that crashed in South Korea last month failed to record the final four minutes of the flight before the crash that killed 179 people on board, officials said in a preliminary report.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: White House: Colombia to take deported migrants after Trump showdown
item.description: The White House said a U.S.-Colombia agreement had come together in the wake of a back-and-forth over immigration and tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Takeaways from AP's reporting on the thousands disappeared in Colombia, Peru and Paraguay
item.description: Thousands of people have disappeared in Latin America during decadeslong conflicts. Many have never been found, presumed to be the victims of dictatorships, insurgencies or organized crime.
item.dc:creator: MARÍA TERESA HERNÁNDEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Belarus strongman wins a 7th term in an election the opposition calls a farce
item.description: The smiling face of President Alexander Lukashenko gazed out from campaign posters across Belarus on Sunday as the country held an orchestrated election virtually guaranteed to give the 70-year-old autocrat yet another term on top of his three decades in power.
item.dc:creator: YURAS KARMANAU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Colombia to send presidential plane to Honduras to pick up migrants from US flights
item.description: President Gustavo Petro has arranged for the presidential plane to facilitate the "dignified return of Colombian nationals "
item.dc:creator: Elvia Limon, NewsNation

item.title: 13 UN peacekeepers, allied soldiers dead in Congo as M23 rebels make gains in key city
item.description: The Rwanda-backed rebel group M23 is battling Congolese government forces in Congo's key city of Goma, causing the international airport there to be evacuated.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A child's life in Gaza, as explained by a Middle East reporter
item.description: Middle East reporter Arwa Damon, who founded the nonprofit INARA, described what she's seen in Gaza to NewsNation.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: In ‘Hail Mary’ move, ICC prosecutor files surprise application as Senate set to vote on sanctioning court
item.description: As the ICC prosecutor files applications for warrants against the Taliban, the Senate is set to vote on sanctions in bill that would trigger action among other things if US citizens, allies are prosecuted.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ukrainian human rights lawyer, Nobel Prize winner urges Trump to show strength and end the war
item.description: Ukrainian human rights attorney and Nobel Peace Prize winner Oleksandra Matviichuk urges President Trump to show strength as he tries to bring the Russia-Ukraine war to an end.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump wants Egypt and Jordan to take in Palestinians from Gaza. Here's why they are likely to refuse
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — President Donald Trump's suggestion that Egypt and Jordan take in Palestinians from the war-ravaged Gaza Strip is likely to be met with a hard “no" from the two U.S. allies and the Palestinians themselves, who fear Israel would never allow them to return.
item.dc:creator: JOSEPH KRAUSS, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Israeli victims of terror concerned with murderers' release from prison, relieved 7 hostages back home
item.description: The release of hostages in exchange for convicted terrorists under Israel’s ceasefire deal with Hamas stirs profound emotions, reigniting debates over justice, security and national unity.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Argentina's Milei rails against wokeism, transgenderism in explosive speech at Davos
item.description: Argentinian President Javier Milei called out the radical left for imposing gender ideology on children, during a controversial speech at the World Economic Forum in Switzerland.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Teen who killed 3 girls at Taylor Swift-themed dance class in England sentenced to over 50 years
item.description: LONDON (AP) — A teenager who stabbed three young girls to death at a Taylor Swift-themed dance class in England was sentenced Thursday to more than 50 years in prison for what a judge called “the most extreme, shocking and exceptionally serious crime.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Tabloids apologize to Prince Harry, admit intruding on Princess Diana
item.description: Prince Harry claimed a monumental victory Wednesday as Rupert Murdoch’s U.K. tabloids made an unprecedented apology.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MELLEY and JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: At least 76 people killed in a hotel fire at a ski resort in Turkey
item.description: ANKARA, Turkey (AP) — A fire raged through a 12-story hotel at a popular ski resort in northwestern Turkey early Tuesday during a school holiday, killing at least 76 people — at least two of them when they jumped from the building to escape the flames, officials said.
item.dc:creator: SUZAN FRASER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: White House cheers release of two Americans freed in a swap with Taliban brokered by Biden, Qatar
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A prisoner swap between the United States and Afghanistan's Taliban freed two Americans in exchange for a Taliban figure imprisoned for life in California on drug trafficking and terrorism charges, officials said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, JON GAMBRELL and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, NewsNation
